message:
test(e2e): update concurrent reservation test for advisory model

Updated test_concurrent_reservation_safety to match the advisory file
reservation model:

Before (strict model):
- Expected exactly one agent to get the reservation
- Other agents expected to have conflicts and be denied

After (advisory model):
- All agents are granted their reservations (granted=1)
- First agent has 0 conflicts
- Subsequent agents have 1+ conflicts reported
- Added explicit error check for unexpected exceptions

The advisory model allows overlapping exclusive reservations while surfacing
conflicts to enable coordination between agents. This is more flexible for
multi-agent workflows where agents need visibility into contention rather
than strict blocking.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -927,17 +927,17 @@ async def try_claim(agent_name: str):
             try_claim(red_stone),
         )
 
-        # Exactly one should succeed (granted=1), others should have conflicts
-        granted_count = sum(1 for r in results if r.get("granted", 0) == 1)
-        conflict_count = sum(1 for r in results if r.get("conflicts", 0) >= 1)
-
-        # At least one should have been granted
-        assert granted_count >= 1, "At least one agent should get the reservation"
-        # The rest should have conflicts (unless there was an error)
-        # Total should be 3
-        assert granted_count + conflict_count + sum(
-            1 for r in results if "error" in r
-        ) == 3
+        errors = [r for r in results if "error" in r]
+        assert not errors, f"Unexpected errors: {errors}"
+
+        # Advisory model: overlapping exclusive reservations are still granted,
+        # but conflicts are surfaced to coordinate between agents.
+        assert all(r.get("granted") == 1 for r in results)
+
+        zero_conflict = sum(1 for r in results if r.get("conflicts") == 0)
+        has_conflicts = sum(1 for r in results if r.get("conflicts", 0) >= 1)
+        assert zero_conflict == 1
+        assert has_conflicts == 2
 
 
 # ============================================================================
